text: It is quiet and dimly lit here, as though I have arrived either after hours or before something has begun. The tilted angles and sparse illumination make me feel slightly disoriented and perhaps apprehensive. There is a sense of abandonment, or waiting—for an answer, for company, for meaning to emerge from the shadows and silence. I am aware of my solitude, confronted both by the emptiness and the expectation that lingers in such spaces.
